title: pouteria adolfi-friederici subsp. floccosa
intro: Pouteria adolfi-friederici subsp. floccosa, also known as the 'Pouteria floccosa', is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark and leaves, which contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pouteria adolfi-friederici subsp. floccosa include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the bark and leaves in hot water or using them in combination with other medicinal plants to create teas, tinctures, and ointments.
